note: Generated by scripts/scrape_sts2_wiki_builds.py; re-run to refresh.

builds.title: Ironclad Strength Build
builds.description: The classic Strength scaling build. Stack Strength through Inflame and Demon Form, then unleash devastating Heavy Blade attacks. One of the most consistent and beginner-friendly archetypes in Slay the Spire 2.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Demon Form
card_acquisition_priority.note: Infinite Strength scaling. A single copy wins any long fight.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Inflame
card_acquisition_priority.note: Immediate +2 Strength (3 upgraded). Core ramp card, take every copy offered.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Heavy Blade
card_acquisition_priority.note: Primary win condition. Deals 14 + 3x Strength damage (5x upgraded). Scales absurdly.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Offering
card_acquisition_priority.note: Lose 6 HP to gain 2 Energy and draw 5 cards. Enables explosive turns.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Fight Me!
card_acquisition_priority.note: Deals damage twice while gaining Strength. Strong early-mid game card.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Battle Trance
card_acquisition_priority.note: Draw 3 cards (4 upgraded). Helps find Heavy Blade and key pieces faster.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Shrug It Off
card_acquisition_priority.note: 8 Block + draw a card. Efficient defense that doesn't slow your deck.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Flame Barrier
card_acquisition_priority.note: 12 Block + retaliatory damage. Solid defense that also deals damage to attackers.

best.name: Vajra
best.note: Free +1 Strength from the start of every combat — perfect synergy.

best.name: Burning Blood
best.note: Ironclad's starter relic. 6 HP heal per fight lets you skip resting and upgrade more.

best.name: Bag of Marbles
best.note: Applies Vulnerable to all enemies turn 1, amplifying your Strength-boosted attacks.

good.name: Ornamental Fan
good.note: Free Block on every 3rd attack played — helps survive while ramping Strength.

good.name: Pen Nib
good.note: Every 10th attack deals double damage. Devastating with a charged-up Heavy Blade.

good.name: Meat on the Bone
good.note: Heal 12 HP when ending combat below 50% — extra survivability during Act 1-2 ramp phase.

avoid.name: Velvet Choker
avoid.note: Limits you to 6 cards per turn. Cripples turns where you need Offering + multiple attacks.

avoid.name: Runic Dome
avoid.note: Not seeing enemy intent makes it hard to time defensive vs offensive turns.

avoid.name: Philosopher Stone
avoid.note: +1 Energy is nice but +1 Strength to all enemies compounds with their existing damage.

key_relics_sidebar.name: Burning Blood
key_relics_sidebar.note: At the end of combat, heal 6 HP.

builds.title: Ironclad Barricade Build
builds.description: A defensive powerhouse that converts Block into lethal damage. Barricade keeps your Block between turns while Body Slam deals damage equal to your current Block. The ultimate 'best defense is a good offense' strategy.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Barricade
card_acquisition_priority.note: The build's keystone. Block no longer decays at end of turn. Everything else depends on this.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Body Slam
card_acquisition_priority.note: Deals damage equal to current Block. Your primary and often only win condition.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Impervious
card_acquisition_priority.note: 30 Block for 2 Energy (40 upgraded). The single best Block card in the game.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Shrug It Off
card_acquisition_priority.note: 8 Block + draw 1 card. Efficient Block that cycles your deck toward key pieces.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Stone Armor
card_acquisition_priority.note: Gain Plated Armor, adding passive Block every turn that Barricade preserves.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Second Wind
card_acquisition_priority.note: Exhaust all non-Attack cards in hand, gaining Block for each. Massive Block burst.

card_acquisition_priority.name: True Grit
card_acquisition_priority.note: 7 Block and Exhaust a card. Thins your deck while adding Block.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Iron Wave
card_acquisition_priority.note: 5 Block + 5 damage in one card. Versatile early pickup that does both jobs.

best.name: Ornamental Fan
best.note: Gain 4 Block every 3 attacks. Since Body Slam is an attack, this compounds your Block stack.

best.name: Anchor
best.note: Start every combat with 10 Block. With Barricade, this Block persists and compounds.

best.name: Horn Cleat
best.note: Gain 14 Block at the start of your 2nd turn. Free Block that stacks with Barricade.

good.name: Thread And Needle
good.note: Start combat with 4 Plated Armor. Passive Block every turn that Barricade preserves.

good.name: Meat on the Bone
good.note: Heal 12 HP when ending combat below 50%. Helps in Act 1 before Barricade is found.

good.name: Orichalcum
good.note: Gain 6 Block if you didn't Block this turn. Useful when drawing all attacks.

avoid.name: Cursed Key
avoid.note: Extra Energy is tempting but Curse cards dilute your deck, making it harder to draw Body Slam and Barricade.

avoid.name: Velvet Choker
avoid.note: Block stacking requires playing many cards per turn. 6-card limit is crippling.

avoid.name: Runic Dome
avoid.note: Knowing enemy intent is critical for deciding when to block vs when to Body Slam.

key_relics_sidebar.name: Ornamental Fan
key_relics_sidebar.note: Every time you play 3 Attacks in a single turn, gain 4 Block.

builds.title: Ironclad Exhaust Build
builds.description: Leverage Exhaust synergies to turn your entire deck into an unstoppable engine. Every exhausted card generates Block, draws cards, and with Dead Branch, creates random replacements. Controlled chaos at its finest.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Corruption
card_acquisition_priority.note: Makes all Skills cost 0 but Exhaust. With Dark Embrace, every free Skill replaces itself.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Dark Embrace
card_acquisition_priority.note: Draw 1 card whenever you Exhaust. Creates chain reactions with Corruption.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Feel No Pain
card_acquisition_priority.note: Gain 3 Block (4 upgraded) per Exhaust. Provides massive passive defense.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Fiend Fire
card_acquisition_priority.note: Exhaust your entire hand, deal damage per card. The ultimate finisher for this build.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Offering
card_acquisition_priority.note: Draw 5 cards + 2 Energy. Fuels the engine on critical turns.

card_acquisition_priority.name: True Grit
card_acquisition_priority.note: 7 Block + Exhaust a card. Reliable defense and Exhaust trigger.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Burning Pact
card_acquisition_priority.note: Exhaust 1 card, draw 2 (3 upgraded). Trades bad cards for better options.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Havoc
card_acquisition_priority.note: Play the top card of your draw pile for free, then Exhaust it. More triggers.

best.name: Dead Branch
best.note: THE relic for this build. Every Exhausted card creates a random card in hand, making Corruption generate infinite value.

best.name: Burning Blood
best.note: Ironclad's starter relic. The HP sustain is crucial while assembling the combo in Acts 1-2.

best.name: Strange Spoon
best.note: 50% chance cards won't Exhaust. With Corruption, your Skills sometimes stay in hand — incredible with powerful Skills.

good.name: Charon Ashes
good.note: Whenever you Exhaust a card, deal 3 damage to all enemies. Free AoE just for doing what the build already does.

good.name: Mummified Hand
good.note: Powers cost 0 after playing a Power. Huge for setting up Corruption + Dark Embrace + Feel No Pain.

good.name: Bag of Preparation
good.note: Draw 2 extra cards turn 1. More cards means more fuel for the Exhaust engine.

avoid.name: Velvet Choker
avoid.note: This build wants to play 10+ cards per turn. A 6-card limit is catastrophic.

avoid.name: Snecko Eye
avoid.note: Randomized costs interfere with Corruption making Skills free. High-cost random assignments waste turns.

avoid.name: Runic Pyramid
avoid.note: Retaining hand between turns reduces new draws. This build wants fresh cards every turn to Exhaust.

builds.title: Ironclad Bloodletting Build
builds.description: A high-risk, high-reward self-damage archetype that converts HP loss into Strength via Rupture, then unleashes devastating attacks. Cards that deal damage to the Ironclad are generally more powerful than average, and this build turns that downside into a Strength-scaling machine.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Rupture
card_acquisition_priority.note: Foundation of the build. Each self-damage instance grants permanent Strength. Upgrade doubles efficiency.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Inferno
card_acquisition_priority.note: Deals AoE damage equal to HP lost this combat. Scales naturally with the self-damage strategy.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Tear Asunder
card_acquisition_priority.note: Multi-hit finisher that gains additional strikes for each time you've lost HP. Nuclear with high Strength.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Crimson Mantle
card_acquisition_priority.note: Guaranteed 1 HP loss per turn triggers Rupture passively. Also provides 8 Block (10 upgraded) every turn.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Bloodletting
card_acquisition_priority.note: Lose 3 HP to gain 2 Energy. Only good with draw support but enables explosive combo turns.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Breakthrough
card_acquisition_priority.note: Strong AoE attack that only costs 1 HP. Best early-game damage card for this archetype.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Brand
card_acquisition_priority.note: Free exhaust that also triggers 1 HP loss. Good for deck thinning and Rupture triggers.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Feed
card_acquisition_priority.note: Gain max HP on kill. Offsets self-damage costs and gives a bigger HP pool to work with.

best.name: Self-Forming Clay
best.note: Gain Block whenever you lose HP in combat — perfect synergy with self-damage cards.

best.name: Demon Tongue
best.note: Works amazingly with Bloodletting and Offering, reducing the effective HP cost.

best.name: Centennial Puzzle
best.note: Free draw on the first time you take damage each combat — triggers reliably in this build.

good.name: Burning Blood
good.note: Ironclad's starter relic. 6 HP heal per fight is essential to sustain self-damage costs.

good.name: Vajra
good.note: Free +1 Strength stacks on top of Rupture scaling.

good.name: Meat on the Bone
good.note: Heal 12 HP when ending combat below 50% — likely in a self-damage deck.

avoid.name: Runic Dome
avoid.note: You need to see enemy intent to decide when it's safe to lose more HP.

avoid.name: Philosopher Stone
avoid.note: Enemies gaining +1 Strength compounds with the damage you're already taking from your own cards.

avoid.name: Coffee Dripper
avoid.note: Losing the ability to rest is dangerous when your build drains your own HP.

key_relics_sidebar.name: Self-Forming Clay
key_relics_sidebar.note: Whenever you lose HP in combat, gain 3 Block next turn.

key_relics_sidebar.name: Burning Blood
key_relics_sidebar.note: At the end of combat, heal 6 HP.

builds.title: Ironclad Strike Build
builds.description: A straightforward offensive deck that utilizes cards containing 'Strike' to power up Perfected Strike for massive single-hit damage. Easy to build with minimal synergy requirements, making it an excellent choice for beginners or when other archetypes don't come together.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Perfected Strike
card_acquisition_priority.note: The build's core card. Minimum 18 damage at game start, scaling to 30+ with more Strike cards added.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Hellraiser
card_acquisition_priority.note: Auto-plays Strike cards when drawn. Transforms the deck from manual beatdown to automated damage machine.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Twin Strike
card_acquisition_priority.note: 10 damage for 1 Energy, benefits from Strength on each hit. Counts as a Strike for Perfected Strike.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Pommel Strike
card_acquisition_priority.note: Decent damage plus card draw. The draw can trigger Hellraiser chains for extra free damage.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Expect a Fight
card_acquisition_priority.note: Extra Energy generation is critical when Perfected Strike costs 2 Energy per play.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Colossus
card_acquisition_priority.note: Supplementary defensive tool. This deck otherwise lacks Block, so Colossus fills the gap.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Taunt
card_acquisition_priority.note: Upgraded provides 2 Vulnerable. Playing this before Perfected Strike adds significant burst damage.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Pyre
card_acquisition_priority.note: Extra Energy gives flexibility to play Perfected Strike and still have Energy for defense.

best.name: Strike Dummy
best.note: Obvious synergy — increases damage of all Strike cards. The perfect relic for this build.

best.name: Bag of Marbles
best.note: Vulnerable on all enemies turn 1 makes your Perfected Strike hits even bigger.

best.name: Pen Nib
best.note: Every 10th attack deals double damage. Devastating when it lines up with Perfected Strike.

good.name: Vajra
good.note: +1 Strength benefits every Strike card, especially multi-hit ones like Twin Strike.

good.name: Ornamental Fan
good.note: Free Block every 3 attacks. Helps shore up this deck's defensive weakness.

good.name: Intimidating Helmet
good.note: Extra Block on turns you play expensive cards like Perfected Strike.

avoid.name: Velvet Choker
avoid.note: Limits cards per turn, which conflicts with Hellraiser chains and multi-Strike turns.

avoid.name: Runic Dome
avoid.note: This deck lacks good defense — you need to see enemy intent to know when to Block.

avoid.name: Cursed Key
avoid.note: Adding Curses dilutes the deck, reducing Perfected Strike consistency and clogging draws.

key_relics_sidebar.name: Burning Blood
key_relics_sidebar.note: At the end of combat, heal 6 HP.

builds.title: Ironclad Self-Wound Build
builds.description: Turn self-inflicted damage into a devastating advantage. Rupture converts every HP lost into permanent Strength, while Tear Asunder gains extra hits for each HP lost in combat. A high-risk, high-reward archetype that weaponizes Ironclad's massive HP pool.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Rupture
card_acquisition_priority.note: Gain 1 Strength whenever you lose HP from a card. The entire build's engine. Take immediately.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Tear Asunder
card_acquisition_priority.note: 5 damage + extra hit per HP lost this combat. Primary damage payoff that scales with self-damage.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Offering
card_acquisition_priority.note: Lose 6 HP for 2 Energy + draw 5. Triggers Rupture and enables explosive turns.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Brand
card_acquisition_priority.note: 0 cost: Exhaust 1 card, lose 1 HP, gain 1 Strength. Deck trimming + Rupture trigger + Strength.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Bloodletting
card_acquisition_priority.note: Lose 3 HP, gain 2 Energy. Triggers Rupture and funds expensive plays.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Hemokinesis
card_acquisition_priority.note: Lose 2 HP, deal 15 damage. Solid damage that also triggers Rupture.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Feed
card_acquisition_priority.note: Deal 10 damage. If it kills, gain 3 max HP permanently. Sustain for a build that loses HP frequently.

card_acquisition_priority.name: Flame Barrier
card_acquisition_priority.note: 12 Block + retaliatory damage. Critical defense for a build with consistently low HP.

best.name: Self-Forming Clay
best.note: Gain 3 Block next turn after losing HP. Since you lose HP constantly, this provides free passive defense every turn.

best.name: Demon Tongue
best.note: At the end of your turn, if you lost HP this turn, gain 1 Strength. Stacks with Rupture for double Strength gain.

best.name: Centennial Puzzle
best.note: Draw 3 cards whenever you lose HP. Free card draw on a build that loses HP every turn.

good.name: Meat on the Bone
good.note: Heal 12 HP if below 50% at end of combat. Insurance against aggressive self-damage.

good.name: Burning Blood
good.note: Ironclad's starter relic. 6 HP heal per fight helps offset the constant self-damage.

good.name: Vajra
good.note: +1 Strength at combat start. Multiplied by Tear Asunder's many hits.

avoid.name: Philosopher Stone
avoid.note: Enemies gain +1 Strength while you're already at low HP. Too dangerous.

avoid.name: Runic Dome
avoid.note: You need to know when enemies hit hard because your HP is often low. Blind intent is suicidal.

avoid.name: Cursed Key
avoid.note: Opening chests costs 1 HP + adds Curses. Extra HP loss without Rupture benefit, and Curses bloat the deck.

key_relics_sidebar.name: Self-Forming Clay
key_relics_sidebar.note: Whenever you lose HP in combat, gain 3 Block next turn.

key_relics_sidebar.name: Demon Tongue
key_relics_sidebar.note: The first time you lose HP on your turn, heal HP equal to the amount lost.
